content.text:
Respond to the question:
How did you feel when you fell behind other players in the Monopoly game? How did you manage your emotions?
回答問題：
當你在地產大亨遊戲中落後其他玩家時，你有什麼感受？你是怎麼調整自己的心情的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	When I fell behind the other players, I initially felt very frustrated and anxious about losing the game. It was discouraging to see them building hotels while I was struggling to pay rent. To manage my emotions, I took a deep breath and reminded myself that it is just a game meant for fun. I shifted my focus from winning to enjoying the interaction with my friends, which completely relaxed me.|I felt a bit helpless and stressed when I realized I was losing early in the Monopoly game. I kept landing on expensive properties and running out of cash quickly. To adjust my mood, I decided to change my strategy and treat the situation as a learning experience instead of a competition. By making jokes about my bad luck, I turned a negative situation into a joyful moment for everyone.|Falling behind in the Monopoly game made me feel disappointed, especially because I had started with a great strategy. However, I managed my emotions by staying patient and waiting for the right opportunity to negotiate trades. I told myself that board games often involve a lot of luck, so I shouldn't take the outcome too seriously. This positive mindset helped me stay engaged and enjoy the rest of the game.
9	When I fell behind, I felt very frustrated and anxious about losing. It was discouraging to see them building hotels while I struggled to pay rent. To manage my emotions, I took a deep breath and reminded myself it is just a game. I shifted my focus to enjoying the interaction with my friends, which relaxed me.|I felt helpless and stressed when I was losing early in the Monopoly game. I kept landing on expensive properties and running out of cash. To adjust my mood, I decided to change my strategy and treat it as a learning experience. By making jokes about my bad luck, I turned it into a joyful moment.|Falling behind made me feel disappointed, because I started with a good strategy. I managed my emotions by staying patient and waiting for the right opportunity to trade. I told myself board games involve luck, so I shouldn't take it too seriously. This mindset helped me enjoy the game.
8	When I fall behind, I feel frustrated and worry about losing. It is sad to see them build houses while I have no money. To manage my emotions, I take a deep breath and think it is just a game. I focus on playing with friends and feel relax.|I feel stressed when I lose early in the Monopoly game. I land on expensive places and lose cash. To adjust my mood, I change my strategy and learn from it. I make jokes about my bad luck and everyone feel happy.|I feel disappointed when I fall behind. I manage my emotion by being patient and wait for good trade. I tell myself games have luck, so do not be too serious. I stay positive and enjoy the game.
7	When I fall behind, I feel angry and sad. They have many houses but I have no money. I take deep breath to manage emotion. I tell myself it is game and play with friends is fun.|I feel sad when I lose game early. I lose money fast. I adjust my mood by make jokes. I laugh at my bad luck and feel better.|I am disappoint when I lose. I stay patient to manage emotion. Game is about luck. I tell myself not serious and just enjoy.
6	I feel angry when fall behind. They have house, I no money. I take breath. Tell myself is game. Play with friend fun.|I sad when lose early. Lose money. I make joke to adjust mood. Laugh my bad luck. Feel better.|I disappoint when lose. Stay patient. Game is luck. Not serious, just enjoy game.
5	I angry fall behind. No money. Take breath. It is game. Play friend fun.|I sad lose early. Make joke adjust mood. Laugh bad luck. Better.|I disappoint lose. Game luck. Not serious. Enjoy game.
4	Angry fall behind. No money. Is game. Friend fun.|Sad lose. Make joke. Laugh bad luck.|Disappoint. Game luck. Not serious.
3	Angry lose. No money. Game.|Sad. Joke. Bad luck.|Disappoint. Luck. Enjoy.
2	Angry. Lose.|Sad. Joke.|Disappoint.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Did you notice any emotional changes in other players during the Monopoly game? Please give one example.
回答問題：
你有注意到其他玩家在地產大亨遊戲中的情緒變化嗎？請描述一個例子。
content.assessment_standard:
10	Yes, I definitely noticed emotional changes in my friends during the Monopoly game. For example, one of my friends became extremely excited and loud when he finally completed a monopoly set. However, his mood quickly shifted to visible frustration when he landed on my most expensive property on the very next turn. It was interesting to observe how the game's unpredictable nature strongly affected his emotional state.|I observed significant emotional shifts in another player who was initially very confident and relaxed. As the Monopoly game progressed and she started losing money, her facial expressions became tissue, and she stopped talking as much. She even sighed heavily when she had to mortgage her favorite properties to pay rent. This situation showed me how competitive pressure can easily change a person's behavior.|During our Monopoly game, I noticed a clear emotional change in a player who was struggling at the beginning. He looked bored and almost wanted to quit because of his bad luck with the dice. But after he successfully negotiated a great trade, his eyes lit up and he became highly engaged and cheerful. It was fascinating to see how a single positive interaction completely transformed his attitude towards the game.
9	Yes, I noticed emotional changes in my friends during the Monopoly game. For example, one friend became extremely excited when he completed a monopoly. However, his mood quickly shifted to frustration when he landed on my expensive property next turn. It was interesting to observe how the game affected his emotional state.|I observed emotional shifts in another player who was initially very confident. As she started losing money, her expressions became tense, and she stopped talking. She sighed heavily when she had to mortgage properties to pay rent. This showed me how pressure can change behavior.|I noticed a clear emotional change in a player who was struggling at the beginning. He looked bored and wanted to quit because of bad luck. After he negotiated a great trade, his eyes lit up and he became cheerful. A positive interaction transformed his attitude.
8	Yes, I notice emotional changes in my friends. For example, my friend become very excited when he get a monopoly. But his mood change to angry when he land on my expensive property. The game really affect his emotion.|I see emotional shifts in a player who is confident at first. When she lose money, her face become tense and she stop talking. She sigh when she mortgage her property. The game pressure change her behavior.|I notice emotional change in a player who play bad at beginning. He look bored and want to quit. But after he make a good trade, he become happy and engaged. A good trade change his attitude to the game.
7	Yes, I notice my friend's emotion change. He is very excited when he get all colors. But he feel angry when he pay me a lot of rent. The game make his emotion change fast.|I see a player change emotion. She is happy first. But she lose money and look angry. She don't talk and sigh. The game make her stress.|I see a player bored at first. He have bad luck. Then he trade property and become very happy. The game is interesting.
6	Yes, friend emotion change. He excited get all color. Then angry pay me rent. Game change emotion fast.|Player change emotion. Happy first. Lose money and angry. Don't talk. Game make stress.|Player bored first. Bad luck. Then trade property and happy. Game interesting.
5	Friend emotion change. Excited get color. Angry pay rent. Emotion change fast.|Player change emotion. Happy then lose money. Angry and no talk. Stress.|Player bored. Bad luck. Trade property happy. Interesting.
4	Emotion change. Excited color. Angry pay. Fast.|Change emotion. Happy lose money. Angry no talk.|Bored bad luck. Trade happy.
3	Emotion change. Excited. Angry.|Happy then lose. Angry.|Bored. Trade happy.
2	Emotion. Angry.|Happy. Lose.|Bored. Happy.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please share a time when you had to negotiate or bargain during a Monopoly game. What strategies did you use to persuade the other player?
回答問題：
請分享一次你在地產大亨遊戲中進行「協商或談判」的經驗，你用了什麼方法說服對方？
content.assessment_standard:
10	In a recent Monopoly game, I negotiated a trade to get the last property I needed for a color set. I offered the other player a property they needed plus some extra cash to make it a win-win situation. I explained how this trade would help them build houses faster than before. By showing them the mutual benefits, I successfully convinced them to agree to the deal.|I remember a time when I had to bargain with a player who wanted a very high price for a property. Instead of arguing, I calmly pointed out that holding the property wasn't generating much rent for them. I offered a fair price and promised immunity from rent for the next three turns. This creative compromise persuaded them to accept my offer.|During a Monopoly game, two of us needed the exact same property to complete our monopolies. I decided to negotiate a complex three-way trade involving another player to get what I needed. I persuaded them by analyzing the board and showing how my proposed trade would weaken the current leading player. This logical strategy successfully convinced everyone to agree.
9	In a Monopoly game, I negotiated a trade to get the property I need. I offered the other player a property they want plus some cash to make a win-win situation. I explained how this trade help them build houses faster. By showing the mutual benefits, I convinced them to agree.|I had to bargain with a player who wanted high price for a property. Instead of arguing, I point out that holding the property wasn't making much rent. I offered a fair price and promised no rent for three turns. This compromise persuaded them to accept.
8	In a Monopoly game, I negotiate a trade to get the last property. I offer the other player a property and some cash. I tell them this trade can help them build houses. Showing the benefits make them agree to the deal.|I bargain with a player who want high price. I tell them holding the property is not good for rent. I give a fair price and promise no rent. This make them accept my offer.
7	I negotiate a trade in the Monopoly game to get property. I give them a property and some money. I tell them it is good for them. Then they agree to trade with me.|I talk with a player for a property. I tell him the property not make money. I give him money and no rent. He agree with me.
6	I negotiate trade in Monopoly game. I give property and money. I say it is good. They agree with me.|I talk to player for buy property. I give money. I say no rent. He say yes.
5	I trade in Monopoly game. Give property and money. It is good. They say yes.|I buy property. Give money and no rent. He say yes.
4	I trade property. Give money. Very good. They yes.|Buy property. No rent. He yes.
3	Trade property. Give money. Yes.|Buy property. No rent.
2	Trade. Money.|Buy property.
1	(Complete silence / No response)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please describe a recent real-life situation where you had to negotiate with someone (For example: part-time jobs, schoolwork, communicating with family). How did you express your needs or state your position?
回答問題：
請描述最近一次你在生活中需要跟他人協商的情況(例如：打工、課業、與家人溝通)，你是如何提出你的需求或立場的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	Recently, I had to negotiate with my group members for a university project because we had a scheduling conflict. Everyone was busy, so I initiated a meeting to calmly discuss our available times and workload. I stated my position clearly by explaining my part-time job schedule and offering to take on more independent tasks. By communicating respectfully, we found a flexible solution that worked for everyone.|I negotiated with my parents last week regarding my curfew because I needed to stay late at the library for finals. I expressed my needs by first acknowledging their concerns about my safety and well-being. Then, I presented a clear plan, including my transportation arrangements and promising to text them when leaving. This responsible approach persuaded them to extend my curfew.|I recently had to bargain with my manager at my part-time job to adjust my shifts. I needed more time to prepare for my midterm exams without losing too many working hours. I approached my manager during a quiet time, explained my situation politely, and suggested swapping shifts with a coworker who had already agreed. My manager appreciated my proactive problem-solving and approved the request.
9	Recently, I had to negotiate with group members for a project because we had a scheduling conflict. I started a meeting to discuss our times and workload. I stated my position by explaining my job schedule and offering to do more independent tasks. By communicating respectfully, we found a solution for everyone.|I negotiated with my parents regarding my curfew because I needed to stay late at the library. I expressed my needs by acknowledging their concerns about my safety. Then, I presented a clear plan with transportation and promised to text them. This approach persuaded them to extend my curfew.|I had to bargain with my manager to adjust my shifts. I needed more time for exams without losing working hours. I approached my manager, explained my situation politely, and suggested swapping shifts with a coworker. My manager appreciated it and approved the request.
8	I have to negotiate with group members for a project because we are busy. I make a meeting to discuss our times. I explain my job schedule and offer to do some tasks alone. We talk respectfully and find a good solution.|I negotiate with my parents about my time to go home. I need to stay at the library late. I tell them my needs and say I will be safe. I give them a plan and promise to text them. They agree to give me more time.|I bargain with my manager for change my shifts. I need time for exams. I talk to my manager politely and say I can swap shifts with my friend. My manager like my idea and say yes.
7	I negotiate with my group for homework. We are very busy. I tell them my job schedule and I can do homework alone. We find a good way together.|I talk to my parents for go home late. I need read books at library. I tell them I will take bus safe and text them. They say yes to me.|I talk with manager to change work time. I have test next week. I tell manager I can change time with my friend. Manager agree.
6	I talk with group for homework. We busy. I tell my job time. We find good way.|I tell parents I go home late. I read at library. I say I take bus safe. They say yes.|I ask manager change time. I have test. I change with friend. Manager say yes.
5	I talk group for homework. Tell my time. We do good.|Tell parents go home late. Study library. Take bus safe. They yes.|Ask manager change work. Have test. Change friend. Manager yes.
4	Talk group homework. Tell time. Good.|Tell parents home late. Study. Safe.|Ask manager change. Test. Friend.
3	Talk group. Time. Good.|Parents home late. Safe.|Manager change. Test.
2	Talk group.|Parents.|Work.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
When negotiating, which area do you feel you need to improve the most? (Listening, communication, compromise, emotional regulation) Why?
回答問題：
在協商時，你覺得自己最需要加強哪一部分？(聆聽、表達、妥協、情緒管理)為什麼？
content.assessment_standard:
10	I feel that I need to improve my emotional regulation the most when negotiating with others. Whenever a discussion becomes intense or someone disagrees with me, I tend to get anxious and defensive. This emotional reaction can make it difficult for me to think clearly and respond professionally. By learning to stay calm, I believe I can achieve much better outcomes in future negotiations.|The area I need to strengthen most is listening, because I often focus too much on my own arguments. While the other person is speaking, I am usually busy planning my next response instead of truly understanding their perspective. Active listening would help me identify their underlying needs and find better mutual solutions. Improving this skill will definitely make my negotiations more successful.|I definitely need to work on my communication and expression skills during negotiations. Sometimes, I have great ideas in my head, but I struggle to articulate them clearly and confidently under pressure. This causes misunderstandings and makes my arguments less persuasive to the other party. Practicing how to state my points logically and assertively will greatly improve my negotiation skills.
9	I feel I need to improve my emotional regulation when negotiating. When a discussion becomes intense, I tend to get anxious and defensive. This emotional reaction makes it difficult for me to think clearly and respond well. By learning to stay calm, I can achieve better outcomes in negotiations.|The area I need to strengthen most is listening, because I focus too much on my own arguments. When the other person is speaking, I am busy planning my response instead of understanding them. Active listening would help me find better solutions. Improving this will make my negotiations successful.|I need to work on my communication skills during negotiations. Sometimes I have great ideas, but I struggle to say them clearly under pressure. This causes misunderstandings and makes my arguments weak. Practicing how to state my points logically will improve my skills.
8	I need to improve emotional regulation when I negotiate. If someone disagree with me, I get anxious and angry. This emotion make it hard to think and talk. If I learn to stay calm, I will have better results.|I need to improve my listening because I think about my own points too much. When people speak, I just plan my answer and don't understand them. Good listening help me find better solutions with others.|I need to improve communication skill. I have good ideas but I cannot say them clearly when I am nervous. This make people misunderstand me. I should practice to say my points better.
7	I need to improve emotional regulation. When people disagree, I feel angry. It make me cannot think clear. I want to learn stay calm for better result.|I should improve listening. I always think my answer when other people talk. I don't listen to them well. If I listen, I can solve problem better.|I need better communication. I have idea but I cannot say clear. People don't understand me. I want to practice talking better.
6	I need improve emotion. I feel angry when disagree. Cannot think clear. Want stay calm.|I need improve listen. I think my answer when people talk. Don't listen well. Listen is important.|I need communication. Have idea but cannot say. People no understand. I want practice.
5	Improve emotion. I angry when talk. No think clear. Need calm.|Improve listen. I think my answer. No listen people. Listen good.|Improve communication. Have idea no say. People no understand.
4	Improve emotion. I angry. No think.|Improve listen. No listen. Think answer.|Improve talk. No say idea. People no know.
3	Emotion. Angry.|Listen. Think.|Talk. Idea.
2	Emotion.|Listen.|Talk.
1	(No response / Silence)
